_meta.description: Tool-access matrix and scheduling rules for the AI-agent office's external tool usage. notebooklm/stitch/base44/google_ai_studio are creative tools with NO unattended API — agents DECIDE and QUEUE tool-tasks (asset-pipeline board entry + asset-task issue spec), a human executes the work in the real tool, and the returned asset comes back into the repo for agent testing/optimization. This config is read by agent-runner.js's tool_task_window block (see daily-schedule.json) to decide, each Sun-Thu sim day, which agent (if any) may open a new tool-task. notebook_x (added 2026-07-01) is the one exception — it DOES have a live, unauthenticated REST API and IS called programmatically per-case, not via the tool-task queue. See its own entry below.
_meta.human_in_the_loop: Agents never call notebooklm/stitch/base44/google_ai_studio programmatically. 'Opening a tool-task' means: append a row to agents/reports/asset-pipeline/board.json (status: queued) and write an asset-task spec file under agents/reports/asset-pipeline/issues/. A human picks it up, does the work in the real tool, and updates the board row to 'returned' with a path/link to the asset. Agents then move it through tested -> optimized -> implemented.

notebook_x.description: As of the 2026-07-18 Q&A-engine rebuild (see CLAUDE.md): agents ask real questions directly to a specific Notebook-X notebook (workers/notebookx-client.js) as ONE of the two production AI systems this office stress-tests — NOT a pre-Claude-escalation reference check anymore (that dual-path 'check notebook-x, fall through to Claude' behavior was retired along with the Netvill CRM case model; see workers/qa-topics.js/qa-engine.js). Each generated question targets exactly one project (data-center/Claude OR notebook-x/Gemini), chosen at generation time. Unlike the four tools below, this is NOT a human-in-the-loop tool-task: no board row, no asset-task issue, no weekly_rotation slot.
notebook_x.auth: none — confirmed via GET /openapi.json: no securitySchemes defined on any endpoint. No NOTEBOOK_X_API_KEY secret needed.
notebook_x.scope_this_integration: Extended 2026-07-18 to cover all 6 notebooks the owner named plus 4 more discovered live in the then-live config/notebook-x-progress.json (deleted 2026-07-18) (kb-cybersecurity, kb-firewall, kb-networking, kb-vpn — all dataQuality:'skeleton' as of 2026-07-09, which makes them GOOD targets for real questions, not a reason to skip them: a skeleton notebook failing a real question is exactly the kind of genuine capability gap this office exists to surface). kb-bash still mirrors kb-linux's trigger (same seeded content, not queried separately) per the original scoping note.

kb_slugs.kb-bash: dataQuality: complete — mirrors kb-linux's trigger, not queried separately (see scope_this_integration)
kb_slugs.kb-cybersecurity: dataQuality: skeleton as of 2026-07-09 — a real target for gap-flagging, not out of scope
kb_slugs.kb-firewall: dataQuality: skeleton as of 2026-07-09 — a real target for gap-flagging, not out of scope
kb_slugs.kb-networking: dataQuality: skeleton as of 2026-07-09 — a real target for gap-flagging, not out of scope
kb_slugs.kb-vpn: dataQuality: skeleton as of 2026-07-09 — a real target for gap-flagging, not out of scope

known_issues: 2026-07-01 verification call: GET /api/knowledge-notebooks and GET /api/notebooks both returned {"notebooks":[]}, and GET /api/health reported {"status":"degraded","notebookCount":0,"githubConnected":false}. Asking kb-linux directly returned {"detail":"GitHub GET notebooks/kb-linux.json: HTTP 401"}. So as of that check, none of kb-linux/kb-bash/kb-1com were actually reachable despite being 'fully seeded per spec' — Notebook-X's own GitHub-backed storage connection needs to be restored on its side before this integration can return real answers. The client/wiring degrade gracefully (return null, treated as a capability-gap signal, not a crash) when this happens — see workers/notebookx-client.js and agent-base.js askNotebookX().

access_by_clearance.note: No gating beyond normal case assignment — any agent handling a linux/1com case may query it, matching this repo's existing pattern of not restricting read-only reference lookups by clearance.

agent_roles.6: primary — builds and extends the project's databases / knowledge centers

notebooklm.goal: The Claude app's AI Search should eventually answer almost exclusively from QA-built NotebookLM knowledge centers (see standing project #1 in asset-pipeline board).

agent_roles.9: joint sessions with Architect — large/high-value tool & UI generation
agent_roles.10: joint sessions with Designer — large/high-value tool & UI generation

stitch.purpose: Generate large, high-value tools/projects (e.g. the Archives app, Designer tooling suite components).

agent_roles.any_admin: may use for small admin-tier product needs
agent_roles.9: most frequent — design tooling suite products
agent_roles.10: most frequent — org products, often paired with Designer

agent_roles.9: joint or independent — generative design/content tooling
agent_roles.10: joint or independent — generative architecture/content tooling

scheduling_rules.description: Applied at the daily 'tool_task_window' block (full_day_schedule, Sun-Thu only — see daily-schedule.json).
general: Use ONLY the four listed tools for content/product generation. Routine cases and information-gathering use the regular models (Gemini for sim, Claude for AI Search) — never burn creative-tool budget on routine cases.
Default product type is a LIGHTWEIGHT app/document unless the spec explicitly calls for something heavier (e.g. Stitch's 'large, high-value' remit).
Stagger usage: no two agents may use the SAME tool in the SAME day's tool_task_window.
Joint sessions (Stitch always; Base44/Google AI Studio when both Designer and Architect opt in) get priority booking over solo sessions for the same tool/day.
An agent may only open a new tool-task if its current open (queued or in-progress) board.json items for that tool are below max_concurrent_per_agent_per_tool.
scheduling_rules.eligibility_check: At each Sun-Thu sim-day start, for the day's assigned tool (per weekly_rotation), check the assigned agent(s)' open board.json items for that tool. If below the concurrency limit AND a standing project (asset-pipeline board) has a 'queued'-eligible next step, open one new tool-task (board row + asset-task spec file). Otherwise, skip silently (no API cost either way — this is a D1/file read + conditional file write, not a Gemini/Claude call).

weekly_rotation.description: 5-day Sun-Thu rotation, one tool-task slot per day, staggered across tools and agents so no two agents compete for the same tool on the same day.
